assertions.title: Resolver status mapped to public values

steps.action: POST /products to create product with GTIN 0012345678905, serialNumber SER-RESOLV-001, category Jewelry
steps.observed: Product created with id cmmbs91me00058y14cd6v20r4, status DRAFT
Product minted, status ACTIVE, txHash and passport populated
Response has "galileo:status": "verified" and "@type": "IndividualProduct" — raw ACTIVE status is never exposed

evidence.network: POST /auth/login -> 200, POST /products -> 200, POST /products/:id/mint -> 200, GET /01/:gtin/21/:serial -> 200 with application/ld+json

assertions.title: GTIN 14-digit padding in resolver URI

steps.expected: Product created; digitalLink and DID use 14-digit padded GTIN
steps.observed: Product created. did: 'did:galileo:01:00012345678905:21:SER-RESOLV-001', digitalLink: 'https://id.galileoprotocol.io/01/00012345678905/21/SER-RESOLV-001' — both correctly use 14-digit padded GTIN

steps.expected: 200 with JSON-LD containing 14-digit padded GTIN in @id, gs1:gtin, digitalLink
steps.observed: 200 OK. @id='did:galileo:01:00012345678905:21:SER-RESOLV-001', gs1:gtin='00012345678905', galileo:digitalLink uses 14-digit padded GTIN. All correct.

steps.expected: 200 with same JSON-LD (resolver accepts both 13 and 14-digit)
steps.observed: 404 NOT_FOUND — resolver does NOT accept 14-digit padded GTIN. DB stores original 13-digit GTIN and resolver uses raw URL param for lookup without trying padded/unpadded alternatives.

assertions.issues: Resolver route in resolve.ts uses `gtin: rawGtin` for DB lookup (line: findUnique where gtin_serialNumber: { gtin: rawGtin }). Since DB stores original GTIN length (13 digits), looking up with 14-digit padded GTIN fails. The contract requires 'Resolver accepts both padded and unpadded'. Fix: resolver should try lookup with both rawGtin and padGtin14(rawGtin) (or strip leading zeros to match DB), or store GTIN as 14 digits in DB.

steps.action: GET /01/00012345678901/21/SER-RESOLV-001 (14-digit GTIN with invalid check digit, last digit changed from 5 to 1)
GET /01/0012345678901/21/SER-RESOLV-001 (13-digit GTIN with invalid check digit)
GET /01/0012345678905/21/SER-RESOLV-001 (13-digit GTIN with valid check digit)
steps.observed: 200 with full JSON-LD response — check digit validation passed, product resolved successfully

assertions.title: JSON-LD custom context for Galileo terms

steps.expected: @context includes schema.org (@vocab), gs1 namespace (ref.gs1.org/voc/), and galileo namespace (galileoprotocol.io/ns/)
Galileo terms (did, digitalLink, txHash, tokenAddress, chainId, mintedAt, status, serialNumber) prefixed with 'galileo:'
steps.observed: All Galileo terms correctly prefixed: galileo:status, galileo:serialNumber, galileo:digitalLink, galileo:did, galileo:passport, galileo:txHash, galileo:tokenAddress, galileo:chainId, galileo:mintedAt

steps.expected: Standard terms use schema.org (unprefixed) and gs1 vocabulary
steps.observed: name, description, category are unprefixed (schema.org via @vocab). gs1:gtin uses gs1 prefix. @type='IndividualProduct' uses schema.org.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-SEC-C3, VAL-GS1-GAP2, VAL-DID-DID2), 1 failed (VAL-GS1-GAP1: resolver does not accept 14-digit padded GTIN for lookup — DB stores original 13-digit GTIN and resolver uses raw URL param without normalization, so GET /01/00012345678905/... returns 404 while GET /01/0012345678905/... returns 200). The 14-digit padding works correctly in the response output (digitalLink, @id, gs1:gtin all use padded form), but the lookup path is broken for padded input.
